extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions.

Key observations on how sent and opted_out can change:
- sent := true occurs ONLY in transition [t2] (send_attempt), which is guarded by `not opted_out`. So the moment sent becomes true, opted_out is necessarily false; that state satisfies the invariant.
- opted_out := true occurs ONLY in [t1] (optout), which simultaneously sets sent := false. So immediately after opted_out becomes true, sent is false — invariant holds.
- opted_out is monotonic: no transition ever assigns opted_out := false, so once true it stays true.

While opted_out is true, every transition that touches sent forces it false:
  - optout [t1]: sent := false
  - send_attempt with opted_out true selects [t3] (not [t2], whose guard fails): sent := false
  - tick [t4]: sent := false
And no transition can set sent := true while opted_out is true, because the only sent-setting transition [t2] requires not opted_out. The initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false) also trivially satisfies the implication.

Therefore in every reachable state, opted_out true implies sent false. No execution (within or beyond 12 events) can reach opted_out=true with sent=true. The property holds; no counterexample exists.
